Cannes Film Festival Workers Planning Protests & Potential Strike Action Over Pay|date=May 2024 |publisher=Deadline}} Few days before the opening ceremony, festival workers called for a general strike. The Broke Behind the Screens ({{lang|fr|Sous les écrans la dèche}}) collective made public a complaint about the precarious nature of film festival work.{{Cite web |last=Roxborough |first=Scott |date=6 May 2024 |title=Cannes Festival Workers Group Calls for Strike Action |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/cannes-festival-workers-calls-for-strike-action-1235890760/ |access-date=6 May 2024 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |language=en-US}} Following the official announcement of ''[[The Seed of the Sacred Fig]]'''s selection for the main competition, Iranian filmmaker [[Mohammad Rasoulof]] was sentenced to eight years in prison as well as flogging, a fine, and confiscation of his property, on the charge of "[[Censorship in Iran|propaganda against the regime]]." Cast and crew were interrogated and pressured to convince Rasoulof to withdraw the film from the festival.{{Cite web |last=Vivarelli |first=Nick |date=2024-05-02 |title=Iran Pressures Mohammed Rasoulof to Pull 'Seed of the Sacred Fig' From Cannes, Slaps Travel Ban on Film's Actors |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/festivals/iran-pressures-mohammed-rasoulof-to-pull-seed-of-the-sacred-fig-cannes-travel-ban-1235989516/ |access-date=2024-05-19 |website=Variety |language=en-US}}{{Cite web |last=Goodfellow |first=Melanie |date=2024-05-08 |title=Mohammad Rasoulof Sentenced To Eight Years In Prison, Flogging & Confiscation Of Property, Says Lawyer |url=https://deadline.com/2024/05/mohammad-rasoulof-sentenced-to-eight-years-prison-flogging-confiscation-1235908664/ |access-date=2024-05-09 |website=Deadline |language=en-US}} Shortly after, Rasoulof and some crew members managed to flee from [[Iran]] to [[Europe]], and attended the film's world premiere on 24 May 2024.{{Cite news |last1=Chrisafis |first1=Angelique |last2=Shoard |first2=Catherine |date=2024-05-13 |title=Iranian film director Mohammad Rasoulof flees Iran to avoid prison |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/article/2024/may/13/iranian-film-director-mohammad-rasoulof-flees-iran-to-avoid-prison |access-date=2024-05-13 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}} On the red carpet, Rasoulof held up images of stars Soheila Golestani and Missagh Zareh, who were unable to leave Iran for the premiere, and had their passport confiscated. The film received a 12-minute standing ovation, while cast and crew protested in solidarity with Iranian women fight for rights.{{Cite web |last1=Setoodeh |first1=Ramin |last2=Shafer |first2=Ellise |date=2024-05-24 |title=Mohammad Rasoulof's 'Seed of the Sacred Fig' Shakes Up Cannes With 2024 Record 12-Minute Standing Ovation, Becoming Palme d'Or Frontrunner |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/festivals/mohammad-rasoulof-seed-of-the-sacred-fig-cannes-standing-ovation-1236015491/ |access-date=2024-05-24 |website=Variety |language=en-US}} The festival opened with French comedy-film ''[[The Second Act (film)|The Second Act]]'' by [[Quentin Dupieux]].{{Cite web |date=April 3, 2024 |title=Quentin Dupieux's new comedy to open the 77th Festival de Cannes |url=https://www.festival-cannes.com/en/press/press-releases/quentin-dupieux-s-new-comedy-to-open-the-77th-festival-de-cannes/ |website=Festival de Cannes}} == Juries == [[File:Greta Gerwig Berlinale 2018.jpg|thumb|216x216px|[[Greta Gerwig]], Main Competition jury president]] [[File:Xavier Dolan Cannes 2015 3.jpg|thumb|217x217px|[[Xavier Dolan]], ''[[Un Certain Regard]]'' jury president]] === Main Competition === * [[Greta Gerwig]], American actress and filmmaker – Jury President * [[J.
